Two packages providing the same logical asset path is a positioned
error naming both declarations; inside fabrik accidental override is
a bug, not a feature.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper => ASSETSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- shared/assets.go --
package shared

import "embed"

//fabrik:assets
//go:embed all:assets
var Assets embed.FS
-- shared/assets/style.css --
body {}
-- web/assets.go --
package web

import "embed"

//fabrik:assets
//go:embed all:assets
var Assets embed.FS
-- web/assets/style.css --
p {}
-- want/diags --
error: web/assets.go:5:1: asset "style.css" is already provided by the tree at $WORK/shared/assets.go:5:1
  help: each asset path belongs to exactly one tree
